meaning: n.(被)安慰,起安慰作用的人或事物
examples: Her appointment was seen as a consolation prize after she lost the election.
The only consolation for the baseball team is that they look likely to get another chance.
If it's any consolation, she didn't get the job, either.
Evenings are spent under the stars: consolation, surely, for not seeing a black rhino.
To have accepted that offer would have been to forgo hatred as consolation and meaning.
